content.text: Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the image pipeline. Let me create a detailed summary: ## Thorough Image Pipeline Analysis Based on my exploration of the Amytis project, here's a detailed breakdown of how images are handled from content to delivery: ### 1. Build Scripts & Image Optimization **Key Build Step** (from `package.json`): ``` "build": "bun scripts/copy-assets.ts && bun run build:graph && next build && next-image-export-optimizer && pagefind --site out" ``` The build process has four distinct phases: 1. **Asset Copy**: Copies images and co-located files from content to public directory 2. **Build Graph**: Generates knowledge graph JSON 3. **Next.js Build**: Builds static HTML pages 4. **Image Optimization**: Processes images through `next-image-export-optimizer` 5. **Search Indexing**: Generates Pagefind search index ### 2. Asset Copy Script (`/scripts/copy-assets.ts`) This is the **critical first step** that prepares images for optimization: **Directory Mapping**: - `content/posts/` → `public/posts/` - `content/series/` → `public/posts/` (series posts get copied as regular posts) - `content/books/` → `public/books/` - `content/flows/YYYY/MM/DD/` → `public/flows/YYYY/MM/DD/` **What It Copies**: - Copies ALL non-markdown files (images, SVGs, assets) - Skips `.md` and `.mdx` files - Preserves directory structure (e.g., `images/` subdirectories) - Uses file modification times to avoid re-copying unchanged files **Posts Support Two Layouts**: 1. **Flat file**: `content/posts/my-post.mdx` (no images) 2. **Nested folder**: `content/posts/my-post/index.mdx` + `images/` subdirectory for co-located images **Example**: A post at `content/posts/2026-01-15-nested-image-test/images/test.svg` gets copied to `public/posts/nested-image-test/images/test.svg` ### 3. Next.js Image Configuration (`next.config.ts`) Uses **static export** with custom image optimization: ```typescript output: "export" // Static site generation images: { loader: "custom" // Use next-image-export-optimizer instead of Next.js default imageSizes: [16, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256, 384] // Thumbnail sizes deviceSizes: [640, 750, 828, 1080, 1200, 1920, 2048, 3840] // Device breakpoints } ``` **Environment Variables** (passed to `next-image-export-optimizer`): - `nextImageExportOptimizer_imageFolderPath`: `"public"` — where to find source images - `nextImageExportOptimizer_exportFolderPath`: `"out"` — where to write optimized output - `nextImageExportOptimizer_quality`: `"75"` — JPEG quality - `nextImageExportOptimizer_storePicturesInWEBP`: `"true"` — convert to WebP format - `nextImageExportOptimizer_generateAndUseBlurImages`: `"true"` — generate blur placeholders ### 4. Image Optimization Output Structure **Location**: `public/images/nextImageExportOptimizer/` For each source image, multiple optimized versions are generated: Example: `amytis-screenshot.jpg` becomes: ``` amytis-screenshot-opt-16.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-32.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-48.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-64.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-96.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-128.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-256.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-384.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-640.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-750.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-828.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-1080.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-1200.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-1920.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-2048.WEBP amytis-screenshot-opt-3840.WEBP ``` All are in WebP format with blur images generated for lazy-loading. ### 5. Image Metadata Plugin (`/src/lib/rehype-image-metadata.ts`) A custom rehype plugin that processes markdown images during rendering: **Path Resolution** (converts relative paths to absolute): - `./images/photo.png` in a post → `/posts/my-post/images/photo.png` - `./images/cover.jpg` in a book → `/books/my-book/images/cover.jpg` - `./images/snap.jpg` in a flow → `/flows/2026/01/15/images/snap.jpg` - `/absolute/path.jpg` → kept as-is - `https://external.com/img.jpg` → kept as-is **Metadata Enrichment** (reads actual dimensions from filesystem): - Uses `image-size` package to read image dimensions - Stores dimensions as `width` and `height` HTML attributes - Only enriches when file exists locally (CDN-only images still get correct URLs) ### 6. CoverImage Component (`/src/components/CoverImage.tsx`) Handles cover images for posts, series, and books: **Two Modes**: 1. **Text Cover** (no image or `text:` prefix): - Shows gradient background with shortened title - Colors based on slug hash (deterministic, multiple palettes) - Supports CJK characters (truncates to 4 chars) and Latin (first 1-2 words) 2. **Image Cover**: - Uses `ExportedImage` from `next-image-export-optimizer` - Accepts CDN base URL via `site.config.ts` - In development: unoptimized (WebP versions don't exist yet) - In production: uses optimized WebP variants **CDN Support** (`getCdnImageUrl`): - Prepends CDN base URL if configured in `site.config.ts` - Example: with CDN `https://cdn.example.com`, `/posts/slug/images/cover.jpg` becomes `https://cdn.example.com/posts/slug/images/cover.jpg` - Skips external URLs, `text:`, and `data:` URIs ### 7. Markdown Image Rendering (`/src/components/MarkdownRenderer.tsx`) Custom `img` component in react-markdown: **Smart Optimization**: ```typescript if (width && height) { // Use ExportedImage with dimensions for responsive sizing return ; } // Fallback: plain without optimization return ; ``` **Development vs. Production**: - **Development**: `unoptimized={true}` (uses original files from public/) - **Production**: Uses WebP variants from `nextImageExportOptimizer/` - External images: Always unoptimized (served as-is) ### 8. Directory Structure Summary **Source** (`content/`): ``` content/ ├── posts/ │ ├── flat-post.mdx │ └── 2026-01-15-nested-image-test/ │ ├── index.mdx │ └── images/ │ └── test.svg ├── books/ │ └── my-book/ │ ├── index.mdx │ └── images/ │ └── cover.jpg └── flows/ └── 2026/ └── 01/ └── 15/ ├── index.mdx └── images/ └── snap.jpg ``` **After Asset Copy** (`public/`): ``` public/ ├── posts/ │ ├── flat-post/ (if nested folder structure used) │ └── nested-image-test/ │ └── images/ │ └── test.svg ├── books/ │ └── my-book/ │ └── images/ │ └── cover.jpg ├── flows/ │ └── 2026/01/15/ │ └── images/ │ └── snap.jpg ├── images/ (site-wide images) │ ├── avatar.jpg │ ├── wechat-qr.jpg │ └── nextImageExportOptimizer/ (all optimized variants) └── nextImageExportOptimizer/ (only used if images in root) ``` **After Build** (`out/`) - exact mirror of `public/` structure ### 9. Image Configuration (`site.config.ts`) **CDN Settings**: ```typescript images: { cdnBaseUrl: "", // Leave empty for local serving // Set to "https://cdn.example.com" or "https://bucket.r2.dev" } ``` When CDN is configured, all local image paths are rewritten at render time to use the CDN URL. ### 10. Key Dependencies - **`next-image-export-optimizer`** (v1.20.1): Handles bulk image optimization to WebP with multiple sizes - **`image-size`** (v2.0.2): Reads image dimensions for responsive sizing - **`ExportedImage`**: React component from `next-image-export-optimizer` for optimized image rendering ### 11. Image Format Support The project supports these image formats: - **Input**: `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.png`, `.gif`, `.webp`, `.svg`, `.avif` - **Output**: WebP (optimized) + original format in output directory - **Optimization**: Quality 75%, blur placeholders enabled ### Summary: Image Flow 1. **Author** creates post at `content/posts/my-post/index.mdx` with `./images/photo.jpg` 2. **Build Script** copies `my-post/images/photo.jpg` → `public/posts/my-post/images/photo.jpg` 3. **Markdown Parser** transforms `./images/photo.jpg` → `/posts/my-post/images/photo.jpg` 4. **Image Optimizer** generates 16 WebP variants in `public/images/nextImageExportOptimizer/` 5. **React Component** renders `` with responsive sizes 6. **HTML Generation** creates `out/posts/my-post/index.html` with references to optimized images 7. **Client** downloads smallest WebP variant matching device size and screen density The pipeline is fully automated and handles responsive image delivery with zero author configuration—images are automatically optimized during the static build phase.

Image Configuration (`site.config.ts`) **CDN Settings**: ```typescript images: { cdnBaseUrl: "", // Leave empty for local serving // Set to "https://cdn.example.com" or "https://bucket.r2.dev" } ``` When CDN is configured, all local image paths are rewritten at render time to use the CDN URL. ### 10. Key Dependencies - **`next-image-export-optimizer`** (v1.20.1): Handles bulk image optimization to WebP with multiple sizes - **`image-size`** (v2.0.2): Reads image dimensions for responsive sizing - **`ExportedImage`**: React component from `next-image-export-optimizer` for optimized image rendering ### 11. Image Format Support The project supports these image formats: - **Input**: `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.png`, `.gif`, `.webp`, `.svg`, `.avif` - **Output**: WebP (optimized) + original format in output directory - **Optimization**: Quality 75%, blur placeholders enabled ### Summary: Image Flow 1. **Author** creates post at `content/posts/my-post/index.mdx` with `./images/photo.jpg` 2. **Build Script** copies `my-post/images/photo.jpg` → `public/posts/my-post/images/photo.jpg` 3. **Markdown Parser** transforms `./images/photo.jpg` → `/posts/my-post/images/photo.jpg` 4. **Image Optimizer** generates 16 WebP variants in `public/images/nextImageExportOptimizer/` 5. **React Component** renders `` with responsive sizes 6. **HTML Generation** creates `out/posts/my-post/index.html` with references to optimized images 7. **Client** downloads smallest WebP variant matching device size and screen density The pipeline is fully automated and handles responsive image delivery with zero author configuration—images are automatically optimized during the static build phase.

1. `bun run build` → runs `next-image-export-optimizer` → generates WebP variants

```
out/images/nextImageExportOptimizer/photo-opt-1080.WEBP
```

- After `bun run build` → you should see both originals and WebP variants in `out/`

If you ran `bun run build` and the `nextImageExportOptimizer/` directories are missing or empty, that would indicate `next-image-export-optimizer` failed silently during the build.
